evidence_quotes.quote: I find it a correct and important step to regulate gambling aspects in games and media applications.
Unity who wants to charge retroactive charges for all software installations.
Many of these digital licences take unilateral rights to change, expand or even switch off the subject matter of the licence without justification.
In some cases even without a special right of notice or reimbursement.

paragraphs.text: I find it a correct and important step to regulate gambling aspects in games and media applications. A lot of emphasis should be placed on this. Preventing ‘dark patterns’ and misleading advertising by influencers would also be a sink and an important step in the right direction. If these aspects were regulated, I would be very happy. Another aspect for me relates to general “unfair commercial practices” regarding digital licensing. Many of these digital licences take unilateral rights to change, expand or even switch off the subject matter of the licence without justification. In some cases even without a special right of notice or reimbursement. Whether this is, for example, ‘Github Copilot’, who suddenly severely limited certain functions despite having concluded an annual licence on the original terms, or Unity who wants to charge retroactive charges for all software installations. Such abrupt changes are absolutely unfair to consumers. Another point that I often encounter is as a ‘purchase’ licence or software loan. This practice is widely used in the Scene game. A game is ‘sold’ here, but manufacturers reserve the right to switch off or even delete the game at any time without having to pay compensation. Thus, in the event of a purchase (I own the product), this would amount to expropriation/theft or damage to property. I would advocate for clear rules and a transparent ownership regime or licence agreements with a clear duration, which cannot be set unilaterally. Thank you for your work!
